content.full_text:
MINASIAN, MINASIAN, MINASIAN, SPRUANCE, BABER, MEITH & SOARES
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
PAUL R MINASIAN, INC.
(A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS)
1681 BIRD STREET
JOHN CARLETON GRAY, 1872-1913
WILLIAM H SPRUANCE, INC
P.O. BOX 1679
CARLETON GRAY, 1893-1944
WILLIAM H. BABER III INC
PAUL JACKSON MINASIAN, 1933-1981
JEFFREY A. MEITH
OROVILLE CALIFORNIA 95965-1679
M. ANTHONY SOARES
TELEPHONE (916) 533-2605
MICHAEL V. SEXTON
DAVID H. MINASIAN
TELECOPIER (916) 533-0197
OF COUNSEL

TIM O'LAUGHLIN

December 28, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Attn: Blanca Breeze

Re: Request for Written Advice – Biggs West Gridley Water
District

Dear Ms. Breeze:

Biggs-West Gridley Water District, a political subdivision of
the State of California, hereby requests written advice regarding
whether the following described situation results in conflict of
interest for District board members.

Biggs-West Gridley Water District is a California water
district formed pursuant to the California Water District law,
Division 13, Water Code §34000 et seq. The District has five
directors. Water Code §34700 states, "Each director shall be a
holder of title to land." Directors are elected at large.

The District diverts water for agricultural purposes from the
Feather River under agreement with the State Department of Water
Resources. The agreement between District and the State contains
a provision entitling the State to reduce deliveries by a
percentage not to exceed fifty percent (50%) in any one year or a
total of 100% in any series of seven consecutive years.

The District contains approximately 34,000 acres. Of this
total, approximately 29,000 acres were part of the original
District; approximately 5,500 acres have annexed to the district
since 1958.

All annexations since 1967 have been approved by Butte County
Local Agency Formation Commission ("LAFCO"). (An annexation of 363
acres in 1958 was prior to LAFCO.) Conditions imposed by LAFCO at
the time of annexation subject annexed lands to prior water rights
which attach to the original district lands.

"Old lands shall have the first right to the
use of water supplied by the District and
canal capacity of the District. At all times

Fair Political Practices Commission
Re: Request for Written Advice - Biggs West Gridley Water
District
December 28, 1990                                                Page 2
----------------------------------------------------------------------

          that there is water and canal capacity not
          required by old lands, the land hereby annexed
          (hereinafter referred to as "new land") shall
          be entitled to use such excess water and canal
          capacity at the same price as is charged to
          old lands.  At all times that there is not
          sufficient water and canal capacity to supply
          the old lands and new lands, the District may
          decline to serve, or limit the service to the
          new lands."

     Additionally, LAFCO further conditioned annexations by
requiring that annexed lands pay for water if additional water is
purchased to provide water to these annexed lands.

          "If it is necessary to purchase water to
          supply the lands within the District, old
          lands shall be entitled to all of the free
          water available under District's water right
          and in such event, the owners of the new land
          shall bear the entire cost of such purchased
          water in addition to the usual rates and
          charges of the District, and such cost of
          purchased water shall be apportioned to the
          new lands in proportion to the amount of water
          used, and the lands included by this order
          shall be charged with their proportionate
          share thereof, based upon quantity of water
          used."

     In 1977, the State imposed a 50% reduction on water deliveries
to the District. Board members at that time decided for a variety
of reasons that all lands within District should be treated the
same. Consequently, irrigation water reductions were borne by all
lands pro rata, without regard to whether said lands were original
District lands or lands which had been annexed subject to the
above-referenced LAFCO conditions.

     Water delivery reductions have not been imposed since 1977.
It appears substantially likely that 1991 may be a year during
which water deliveries are reduced by 50% due to drought.

     Between 1978 and 1985 there were years when the district
purchased additional water supplies in order to irrigate lands when
full rice production was allowed. Water purchased was not paid for
by annexed lands alone, even though the LAFCO condition seems to
require such action.

Fair Political Practices Commission
Re: Request for Written Advice - Biggs West Gridley Water
District
December 28, 1990                                                Page 3
---------------------------------------------------------------------

     Government Code §87100 states, "No public official at any
level of state or local government shall make, participate in
making, or in any way attempt to use his official position to
influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason
to know he has a financial interest."

     A public official makes a governmental decision when he or
she, acting within the authority of his or her office, votes on a
matter (F.P.P.C. Regulations, 2 CCR §18700(b)).  A public official
participates in the making of a governmental decision when, acting
within the authority of his or her position, he or she advises or
makes recommendations to the decision-maker, or presents any
opinion which requires the exercise of judgment on the part of the
public official and the purpose of which is to influence the
decision.  (2 CCR §18700(c)).

     The District's five directors and their land holdings within
the District are as set forth in the table below.  The table
identifies whether the director's land is part of the original
district lands or land subsequently annexed.

┌──────────────┬─────────────┬──────────────┬──────────────┬─────────────┐
│ Director     │ Total Acres │ Original     │ Annexed      │ Year        │
│              │             │ Lands        │ Lands        │ Annexed     │
├──────────────┼─────────────┼──────────────┼──────────────┼─────────────┤
│Edward W.     │ 120.3       │ -0-          │ 120.3        │ 1967        │
│Boeger        │             │              │              │             │
├──────────────┼─────────────┼──────────────┼──────────────┼─────────────┤
│Gary J.       │ 1,435.52    │ 1,280.34     │ 155.18       │ 1967        │
│Rudd          │             │              │              │             │
├──────────────┼─────────────┼──────────────┼──────────────┼─────────────┤
│Earl E.       │ 500.83      │ 43.83        │ 457          │ 1976        │
│King          │             │              │              │             │
├──────────────┼─────────────┼──────────────┼──────────────┼─────────────┤
│Gary N.       │ 4,004.14    │ 2,814.14     │ 1,190        │ 1976-77     │
│Justeson      │             │              │              │             │
├──────────────┼─────────────┼──────────────┼──────────────┼─────────────┤
│John Adams    │ 468.49      │ 468.49       │ -0-          │ N/A         │
└──────────────┴─────────────┴──────────────┴──────────────┴─────────────┘

     The Board requests written advice on whether its members may
discuss and thereafter vote on a District resolution that would
impose contractual water delivery reductions on all lands and not
just on annexed lands notwithstanding LAFCO conditions.  (Whether
or not the Board has the authority to modify District water
deliveries notwithstanding LAFCO imposed conditions is not an issue
before FPPC.)  The Board additionally requests written advice on
whether its members may discuss and thereafter vote on a District
resolution that would result in payment for purchased water by all
lands and not just by annexed lands notwithstanding LAFCO
conditions.

Fair Political Practices Commission
Re: Request for Written Advice - Biggs West Gridley Water
District
December 28, 1990                                                Page 4
___________________________________________________________________

     All directors are financially interested in the decisions: a
director whose lands are included within the original District
lands will likely vote during a water shortage year that LAFCO
imposed conditions on annexed lands should be observed.  The
imposed conditions result in more water being available to original
lands since water deliveries to annexed lands would be cut back
completely in favor of water deliveries to original lands.
Conversely, directors who own lands which are part of annexed lands
would likely vote during a water reduction period to have
reductions imposed upon all lands equally as was done in 1977.

     Likewise, directors who own annexed lands have a financial
interest in a decision which spreads the cost of purchased water
districtwide rather than having only annexed lands bear the entire
cost of purchased water.

     May the directors discuss and vote upon these matters
notwithstanding their financial interest in the decisions?

     The "public generally" exception as set forth in 2 CCR §18703
provides that an official is not disqualified from participation in
a governmental decision if the decision affects the official's
interest in a manner which is not distinguishable from the manner
in which the decision will affect the public generally. In other
words, there is no conflict of interest if the decision affects the
officials' interests in a substantially the same manner as it will
affect all members of the public or a significant segment of the
public. In the instant situation, there are two classes of public.
The first class consists of landowners of approximately 5,000 acres
of lands which have been annexed to the District and are subject to
LAFCO conditions relating to water rights. As four of the five
board members are members of the smaller annexed lands class, does
the "public generally" exception permit them to discuss and vote
upon a decision which could result in imposing water delivery
reductions equally upon the smaller class and the larger class?

     Government Code §1090 prohibits the involvement by a public
official in a decision in which he or she has a financial
contractual interest. If your written advice is that the District
board members may participate in the decisions as described above
under the "public generally" exception, or based on other
reasoning, does Government Code §1090 nevertheless prohibit
District director involvement in the above-described decisions on
the basis that they each have a financial interest in water
deliveries made pursuant to the contract between District and the
State Department of Water Resources and the cost of such water.

Fair Political Practices Commission
Re: Request for Written Advice - Biggs West Gridley Water
     District

December 28, 1990                                          Page 5
___________________________________________________________________

     The District hereby requests written advice on these matters.
Please contact me if you need further information.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        MINASIAN, MINASIAN, MINASIAN,
                                        SPRUANCE, BABER, MEITH & SOARES


                                        BY ________________________
                                           MICHAEL V. SEXTON

MVS:aw
